text: How many players are on a soccer team on the field at one time?
explanation: Each soccer team has 11 players on the field including the goalkeeper.

text: Who was the first President of the United States?
explanation: George Washington became the first U.S. president in 1789.

text: What planet is known as the Red Planet?
explanation: Mars looks red because of iron oxide on its surface.

text: What is the capital of France?
explanation: Paris is the capital and largest city of France.

text: What is the main ingredient in guacamole?
explanation: Guacamole is made primarily from mashed avocado.

text: Which movie features a toy cowboy named Woody?
explanation: Woody is one of the main characters in Toy Story.

text: Which instrument has black and white keys?
explanation: A piano is played using black and white keys.

text: What is the largest land animal?
explanation: Elephants are the largest animals that live on land.

text: In basketball, how many points is a free throw worth?
explanation: A made free throw counts for one point.

text: In which year did World War II end?
explanation: World War II ended in 1945.

text: What gas do humans need to breathe to survive?
explanation: Humans breathe oxygen to stay alive.

text: Which continent is the Sahara Desert located on?
explanation: The Sahara Desert is in northern Africa.

text: What food is traditionally eaten on Thanksgiving in the United States?
explanation: Turkey is the classic Thanksgiving main dish in the U.S.

text: Which superhero is known as the Dark Knight?
explanation: Batman is often called the Dark Knight.

text: Which singer is known as the King of Pop?
explanation: Michael Jackson is famously known as the King of Pop.

text: What animal is known for having a black-and-white striped coat?
explanation: Zebras are known for their black-and-white stripes.

text: How many holes are played in a standard round of golf?
explanation: A standard round of golf includes 18 holes.

text: Who discovered America in 1492?
explanation: Christopher Columbus reached the Americas in 1492.

text: What is H2O commonly known as?
explanation: H2O is the chemical formula for water.

text: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean on Earth.

text: Which country is famous for pizza?
explanation: Pizza is strongly associated with Italy, especially Naples.

text: Which animated movie features a snowman named Olaf?
explanation: Olaf is the cheerful snowman from Frozen.

text: Which band released the album 'Abbey Road'?
explanation: Abbey Road is one of The Beatles' most famous albums.

text: What do bees collect from flowers?
explanation: Bees collect nectar from flowers to help make honey.

text: What part of the human body pumps blood?
explanation: The heart pumps blood throughout the body.

text: What sport uses a bat and bases?
explanation: Baseball is played with a bat and four bases.

text: Which ancient civilization built the pyramids of Giza?
explanation: The ancient Egyptians built the pyramids of Giza.

text: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
explanation: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun.

text: What is the capital of Japan?
explanation: Tokyo is the capital city of Japan.

text: What is cheese mainly made from?
explanation: Cheese is made from milk.

text: Which character lives in a pineapple under the sea?
explanation: SpongeBob SquarePants lives in a pineapple under the sea.

text: Which pop star released 'Shake It Off'?
explanation: Taylor Swift released 'Shake It Off' in 2014.

text: What is the fastest land animal?
explanation: The cheetah is the fastest land animal.

text: In tennis, what is a score of zero called?
explanation: In tennis, zero is called love.

text: Who was the first man to walk on the Moon?
explanation: Neil Armstrong first walked on the Moon in 1969.

text: What is the boiling point of water at sea level in Celsius?
explanation: At sea level, water boils at 100 degrees Celsius.

text: Which country is home to the Great Pyramid of Giza?
explanation: The Great Pyramid of Giza is located in Egypt.

text: What is the main ingredient in hummus?
explanation: Hummus is traditionally made from chickpeas.

text: Who played Iron Man in the Marvel Cinematic Universe?
explanation: Robert Downey Jr. played Iron Man in the Marvel movies.

text: Which band is known for the song 'Bohemian Rhapsody'?
explanation: 'Bohemian Rhapsody' is one of Queen's signature songs.

text: What is the largest shark species?
explanation: The whale shark is the largest shark species.

text: Which country won the FIFA World Cup in 2018?
explanation: France won the 2018 FIFA World Cup.

text: What wall divided Berlin from 1961 to 1989?
explanation: The Berlin Wall divided East and West Berlin.

text: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
explanation: Gold's chemical symbol is Au.

text: What is the capital of Canada?
explanation: Ottawa is the capital of Canada.

text: What is sushi commonly wrapped in?
explanation: Many types of sushi are wrapped in seaweed called nori.

text: Which school does Harry Potter attend?
explanation: Harry Potter attends Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry.

text: Who sang 'Rolling in the Deep'?
explanation: Adele sang the hit song 'Rolling in the Deep'.

text: What is the tallest type of tree in the world?
explanation: Redwoods are the tallest trees in the world.

text: How many strikes make an out in baseball?
explanation: A batter is out after three strikes.

text: Which empire was ruled by Julius Caesar?
explanation: Julius Caesar was a Roman leader.

text: What force pulls objects toward Earth?
explanation: Gravity pulls objects toward Earth.

text: What is the smallest continent by land area?
explanation: Australia is the smallest continent.

text: What is miso traditionally made from?
explanation: Miso is a seasoning made from fermented soybeans.

text: Which movie features a dinosaur theme park that goes wrong?
explanation: Jurassic Park is about a dinosaur park that loses control.

text: Which artist released 'Shape of You'?
explanation: Ed Sheeran released 'Shape of You' in 2017.

text: What is the largest ocean mammal?
explanation: The blue whale is the largest mammal in the ocean.

text: What part of the cell contains most of its DNA?
explanation: Most of a cell's DNA is stored in the nucleus.

text: How many quarters are in an American football game?
explanation: An American football game has four quarters.

text: Who was known as the Maid of Orleans?
explanation: Joan of Arc was called the Maid of Orleans.

text: Which organ filters waste from the blood?
explanation: The kidneys filter waste products from the blood.

text: What is the longest river in the world by traditional measure?
explanation: The Nile is traditionally listed as the world's longest river.

text: Which country is known for tacos?
explanation: Tacos are strongly associated with Mexican cuisine.

text: Who voices the character of Mario in many modern Nintendo games?
explanation: Charles Martinet voiced Mario for many years in Nintendo games.

text: Which artist released the album 'Thriller'?
explanation: Michael Jackson released the album 'Thriller'.

text: What is the process by which plants make food using sunlight?
explanation: Plants use photosynthesis to make food from sunlight.

text: What sport uses a shuttlecock?
explanation: Badminton is played using a shuttlecock.

text: Which country gifted the Statue of Liberty to the United States?
explanation: France gave the Statue of Liberty to the United States.

text: What is the freezing point of water in Celsius?
explanation: Water freezes at 0 degrees Celsius.

text: Which mountain range contains Mount Everest?
explanation: Mount Everest is part of the Himalayas.

text: What is the main ingredient in pesto?
explanation: Traditional pesto is made mainly with basil.

text: Which actor played Jack Dawson in Titanic?
explanation: Leonardo DiCaprio played Jack Dawson in Titanic.

text: Which band is known for the song 'Hotel California'?
explanation: 'Hotel California' is one of the Eagles' best-known songs.

text: What is the largest bird in the world?
explanation: The ostrich is the largest bird in the world.

text: In golf, what is one stroke under par called?
explanation: One stroke under par is called a birdie.

text: Which city was buried by the eruption of Mount Vesuvius in AD 79?
explanation: Pompeii was buried when Mount Vesuvius erupted in AD 79.

text: What is Avogadro's number closest to?
explanation: Avogadro's number is approximately 6.02 x 10^23.

text: Which country borders the most other countries?
explanation: China borders more countries than any other nation.

text: What is the Japanese name for grilled skewered chicken?
explanation: Yakitori is grilled chicken served on skewers.

text: Which film won the Academy Award for Best Picture in 1994?
explanation: Forrest Gump won Best Picture at the Oscars for 1994 films.

text: Which composer wrote 'The Four Seasons'?
explanation: Antonio Vivaldi composed 'The Four Seasons'.

text: What is the only mammal capable of true sustained flight?
explanation: Bats are the only mammals capable of true sustained flight.

text: What is the maximum possible score in a single frame of ten-pin bowling?
explanation: A strike followed by two more strikes gives a frame score of 30.

text: Who was the first U.S. president to live in the White House?
explanation: John Adams was the first U.S. president to live in the White House.

text: What is the approximate speed of light in kilometers per second?
explanation: The speed of light is about 300000 kilometers per second.

text: Which country has the most natural lakes?
explanation: Canada has more natural lakes than any other country.

text: Which cheese is traditionally used in a classic Greek salad?
explanation: Feta cheese is a classic ingredient in Greek salad.

text: Which actor voiced Darth Vader in the original Star Wars trilogy?
explanation: James Earl Jones provided Darth Vader's voice.

text: Which band released the song 'Stairway to Heaven'?
explanation: Led Zeppelin released 'Stairway to Heaven'.

text: What is the largest desert on Earth?
explanation: Antarctica is the largest desert on Earth.

text: What statistic category, along with points and rebounds, often completes a basketball triple-double?
explanation: Assists commonly form part of a triple-double along with points and rebounds.

text: Which ship completed Ferdinand Magellan's circumnavigation after his death?
explanation: The ship Victoria completed the circumnavigation.

text: What is the pH of pure water at room temperature?
explanation: Pure water is neutral with a pH of 7.

text: What is the world's deepest ocean trench?
explanation: The Mariana Trench is the deepest known ocean trench.

text: Which spice is harvested from the dried stigmas of a flower?
explanation: Saffron comes from the stigmas of the crocus flower.

text: Which 1927 film is famous for being the first feature-length movie with synchronized spoken dialogue?
explanation: The Jazz Singer is widely known as the first feature-length talkie.

text: Which jazz musician was nicknamed 'Satchmo'?
explanation: Louis Armstrong was famously nicknamed Satchmo.

text: Which layer of Earth's atmosphere contains the ozone layer?
explanation: Most of Earth's ozone layer is located in the stratosphere.

text: In cricket, how many players are on each team?
explanation: Each cricket team has 11 players.

text: Who was the British prime minister for most of World War II?
explanation: Winston Churchill served as Britain's wartime prime minister for most of World War II.
